campbell.title: Campbell City Council — March 24, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council held its March 24, 2026 meeting to honor K9 Lucas, the police department's first working dog who passed away on March 15, 2026. The council approved several routine items, discussed a beekeeping ordinance after addressing safety concerns, and presented a feasibility study on regional homeless services involving Campbell and four neighboring West Valley cities.
keyTopics: Tribute to K9 Lucas, the retired police dog who served 2019–2023 and passed March 15, 2026
Beekeeping ordinance (second reading approved): county confirmed hive locations are kept confidential under state law
Senate Bill 79 / Transit-Oriented Development: three council members recused due to property conflicts within the half-mile transit zone; rule of necessity invoked
West Valley Homeless Services Feasibility Study: council voted to support a regional implementation task force with Campbell and four neighboring cities
Annual Comprehensive Financial Report: city finance team recognized with award of excellence for financial reporting

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 4, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council met to decide how to distribute grant money to different community groups and events. They approved funding for specific organizations, set aside money for competitive event grants in the upcoming fiscal year, and made changes to the rules for how groups can apply for event funding and street closure permits.
keyTopics: Approved specific organizations to receive grant funding
Set budget for Competitive Event Grant Program for the fiscal year
Updated rules for Community Event and Street Closure Grant applications
Determined individual dollar amounts each approved recipient will receive

san-jose.title: San José City Council — March 25, 2026
san-jose.summary: This was a San José City Council meeting held on March 25, 2026. The meeting focused on providing accessibility options for residents who wanted to watch or participate, including live translations in over 50 languages and multiple ways to join online or by phone.
keyTopics: Live translation services available in 50+ languages
Multiple viewing options: cable, YouTube, website, and phone dial-in
Zoom webinar access for online participation
Meeting accessibility for non-English speakers

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a routine meeting on March 30, 2026, where they approved previous meeting minutes and opened the floor for public comments. The council also reviewed committee reports and set dates for upcoming board and commission meetings throughout the city.
keyTopics: Approval of previous meeting minutes
Public comment period for residents
Committee and staff reports
Scheduling of future board and commission meetings
Recreation Division contact for agenda questions (650-903-6331)

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met to review funding applications for community development grants that help pay for local housing and support programs. The group also approved meeting notes from their previous session in February.
keyTopics: Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) applications for housing and services funding
Review of February 25, 2026 meeting minutes
Housing and Human Services Commission operations

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The city council focused on helping local businesses grow and thrive. They discussed ways to reach out to shops at local malls, forgive unpaid business licenses, plan a summer shopping campaign to support local stores, and offer financial incentives to attract new retail and startup companies to Cupertino.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program - helping businesses catch up on unpaid fees
Shopping center outreach - connecting with mall and plaza businesses
Summer of Celebrations campaign - encouraging residents to shop locally
Public-private partnerships - city partnering with private companies to sponsor events
Economic incentives - offering tax breaks or grants for new retail and startups

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — March 30, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara's City Council held a Governance and Ethics Committee meeting on March 30, 2026. The city is continuing to allow residents to attend meetings either in person or remotely via Zoom or phone.
keyTopics: In-person and remote meeting options available for residents
Zoom participation details (ID: 985 5995 1444)
Phone dial-in option (+1 669 444 9171)
Written public comments accepted via email by 10 a.m. the day before meetings

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met on March 23, 2026 to recognize Women's History Month and approve two agreements: one allowing the American Red Cross to use city facilities during emergencies, and another to purchase email security software from Insight Public Sector for $93,383 per year over three years.
keyTopics: Women's History Month proclamation
American Red Cross disaster relief facility agreement
Email security software purchase ($93,383/year for 3 years)
Late agenda item added to meeting packet
